Event: Smithers robs a jewelry store in the arcade game
Explanation: Gains loot and villain status while harming the store and society.

Event: Smithers wears a cape filled with bombs in the arcade game
Explanation: Equips dangerous weapons that help him fight but endanger others.

Event: Smithers follows Burns in most of his evil schemes despite disliking them
Explanation: Retains position and proximity to Burns but helps enact harmful schemes.

Event: Smithers grows a beard and neglects his appearance from stress over possibly shooting Burns
Explanation: His guilt and anxiety damage his own wellbeing without affecting others.

Event: Smithers becomes haggard and stops shaving from stress over Homer’s beating order
Explanation: Stress and conflicted loyalty only hurt him while others are unaffected at that moment.

Event: Smithers acts as a fair supervisor and rewards employees appropriately
Explanation: He maintains authority and smooth operations while treating employees well.

Event: Smithers bears badgering and punishment from Burns without leaving
Explanation: He suffers repeated mistreatment with little compensating gain shown.

Event: Smithers smokes when extremely upset and anxious
Explanation: His coping mechanism harms his own health without helping anyone.

Event: Smithers hides his sexuality and pines secretly for Burns
Explanation: He represses himself and gets no reciprocal benefit from Burns.

Event: Smithers reminds the scatterbrained Burns who Homer is
Explanation: He stays useful and trusted while helping Burns function.

Event: Smithers gives Burns advice, listens to his troubles, and offers a shoulder to cry on
Explanation: He deepens his bond and role while supporting Burns emotionally.

Event: Smithers ruins Homer’s career when Homer gains Burns’s favor
Explanation: He protects his status with Burns while severely harming Homer’s career.

Event: Smithers forces Marge out of Homer’s life after “winning” Homer in a poker game
Explanation: He gratifies his jealousy and control impulse while severely hurting Marge.

Event: Smithers becomes extremely thankful and kind to those who help Burns or their relationship
Explanation: He strengthens alliances and encourages prosocial acts toward Burns.

Event: Smithers’ parents send him to a burlesque house to “cure” him
Explanation: They try to impose their preference on him, distressing him with little clear gain.

Event: Smithers hides nuclear waste in public areas for Burns
Explanation: He keeps favor with Burns while endangering the public.

Event: Smithers happily helps Lisa track down the creator of Malibu Stacy
Explanation: He builds a positive bond and faces no downside while aiding Lisa’s goal.

Event: Smithers goes on a drunken bender after being fired by Burns
Explanation: He self-destructs from emotional pain without helping anyone.

Event: Smithers goes behind Burns’s back to help remove Sideshow Bob as mayor
Explanation: He risks his position to avert harmful ultra-conservative rule.

Event: Smithers chain-smokes while anxiously discussing Burns’s order to have Homer beaten
Explanation: His only action is self-harming anxiety and smoking with no benefit.

Event: Smithers becomes overly stressed trying to find a temporary replacement who won’t outshine him
Explanation: He protects his own status at the cost of undermining better help for Burns.

Event: Smithers begs to stay when Burns orders him to take a vacation
Explanation: His psychological dependence hurts his own rest and autonomy without helping others.

Event: Smithers repeatedly calls Burns while on vacation asking for work
Explanation: His obsession undermines his own rest and mildly annoys Burns without clear gain.

Event: Smithers objects to some of Burns’s schemes at risk of his own job
Explanation: He risks career harm to curb the worst harms to others.

Event: Smithers refuses to allow Homer to be beaten after he saves Burns’s life
Explanation: He protects Homer from serious harm while preserving some moral integrity.

Event: Smithers insists Burns not leave Bart for dead after hitting him with his car
Explanation: He saves Bart’s life with little shown cost to himself.

Event: Smithers warns Apu, Wiggum, and Hibbert that appealing to Burns’s better nature is dangerous
Explanation: He helps them avoid harm with no cost to himself.

Event: Smithers wants to break free from doing evil tasks and questions his own morality
Explanation: His self-reflection moves him toward moral improvement and reduced guilt.

Event: Smithers quits his job over Burns’s “unconscionable” evil in “Who Shot Mr. Burns?”
Explanation: He sacrifices his own position and identity to take a stand that benefits others.

Event: Smithers ignores Burns’s sexual innuendo about the stolen school oil well
Explanation: He avoids engaging but still enables the theft, gaining only continued proximity.

Event: Smithers refuses to participate in Burns’s plan to block out the sun
Explanation: He risks Burns’s wrath to oppose an extremely harmful scheme.

Event: Smithers carries out most of Burns’s orders despite being an extreme doormat
Explanation: His compliance costs him dignity and ethics while only marginally maintaining status.

Event: Smithers maintains closeness with his mother and gives her a birthday present
Explanation: He nurtures a healthy relationship that benefits both of them emotionally.

Event: Smithers talks Burns out of having Homer beaten for writing an angry letter
Explanation: He protects Homer and shows positive influence over Burns without drawback.

Event: Smithers alphabetizes Burns’s breakfast and enjoys obsessive tidiness
Explanation: His compulsive order both pleases himself and helps Burns’s routines.

Event: Smithers enjoys being demoted to bathroom cleaning duty
Explanation: He finds satisfaction in menial work while still serving workplace needs.

Event: Smithers befriends Marge over shared interests and relationship troubles
Explanation: The friendship provides mutual emotional support and understanding.

Event: Smithers helps Lisa find the creator of Malibu Stacy (reiterated as Punch-Clock Villain example)
Explanation: He assists Lisa’s activism and gets social credit without harm.

Event: Smithers acts as a Benevolent Boss when Burns is arrested until employees mock him
Explanation: He tries to run the plant fairly, benefiting both his leadership role and their treatment.

Event: Smithers is arrested for “tidying federal property”
Explanation: His harmless compulsion leads to legal trouble, hurting himself while not harming others.

Event: Smithers alphabetizes Mr. Burns’ breakfast in “Homer the Smithers”
Explanation: He organizes tasks efficiently, satisfying himself and helping Burns’s routine.

Event: Smithers moves bankrupt Burns into his house and continues to serve him
Explanation: He sacrifices his own resources to care for Burns, who benefits more than he does.

Event: Smithers opposes Burns’s plan to block out the sun as “cartoonish super-villainy”
Explanation: He actively tries to stop catastrophic harm at some risk to his position.

Event: Smithers goes behind Burns’s back to reveal Sideshow Bob’s electoral fraud to Bart and Lisa
Explanation: He endangers his job to protect civil society and his own lifestyle.

Event: Smithers corrects Burns’s idea of using long-deceased veterans for the softball team
Explanation: He prevents an absurd failure while proving his value as a competent advisor.

Event: Smithers offers to make a legal logo for Burns’s cult after he copies copyrighted ones
Explanation: He saves Burns from legal trouble and shows his usefulness.

Event: Smithers sings about his sexual frustration and failed confession to Burns
Explanation: Venting highlights his unmet needs without improving his situation.

Event: Homer sets Smithers up with another man after hearing his woes
Explanation: He helps Smithers pursue a healthier romantic option with no personal loss.
